title: Pachygenium paraguayense

Asphodelaceae is a family of monocots within the order Asparagales. Asparagales is a large order of flowering plants that also includes lilies, orchids, and agaves. Pachygenium paraguayense is native to Paraguay and is a rare and unusual species.

Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing pain and inflammation.
